prompt:
Update the reel-life NixOS configuration in hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix to use new features that were just merged into reel-life.

## Current config location
hosts/classic-laddie/default.nix, around line 115-139, under services.reel-life

## Changes needed

### 1. Add admin chat ID for health alerts
Add this line to the services.reel-life block:
```nix
chatTelegramAdminChatID = 8780088233;
```
This is the same user ID already in chatTelegramAllowedUsers. It routes health alerts to this chat directly, avoiding the startup error when chatTelegramChatID is 0 (auto-capture mode).

### 2. Add persistent history path
Add this line to the services.reel-life block:
```nix
agentHistoryPath = "/var/lib/reel-life/history.json";
```
This persists conversation history across restarts. The directory /var/lib/reel-life/ is already used for notebookPath.

### 3. Update flake.lock to pull latest reel-life
Run:
```
nix flake update reel-life
```
This pulls in the latest reel-life which includes: admin chat ID support, persistent history, Sonarr/Radarr/Prowlarr/Overseerr write tools, and default history size bump to 40.

### 4. Ensure ReadWritePaths includes /var/lib/reel-life
Check if the reel-life NixOS module or the cosmo config already grants write access to /var/lib/reel-life. If the systemd service has ReadWritePaths = [] or doesn't include this path, it needs to be added. The reel-life module may handle this automatically via StateDirectory — check and only add manually if needed.

## Do NOT change
- Do not modify secrets or agenix files
- Do not change any other service configurations
- Do not change chatTelegramChatID (it stays at 0 for auto-capture)

## Testing
Run 'nix flake check' or 'nix build .#nixosConfigurations.classic-laddie.config.system.build.toplevel --dry-run' to verify the config evaluates correctly.
